title: Judicial Status of Major Kirchnerism Corruption Cases
summary: Comprehensive judicial status table for all K-era corruption cases, covering case name, defendants, charges, current status, amounts involved, and connections. Data as of March 2026.

cases.summary: Systematic corruption in the awarding of 51 road construction contracts in Santa Cruz province (2003-2015), directing auctions to benefit Lazaro Baez through Austral Construcciones. Half of the 51 works remained unfinished by Dec 2015. Baez received ARS 24,500 million in public works contracts, representing 11.6% of national road budget directed to Santa Cruz.

defendants.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
defendants.sentence: 6 anos de prision + inhabilitacion perpetua para cargos publicos
defendants.status: Condena firme. Detencion domiciliaria en San Jose 1111, CABA.

defendants.name: Lazaro Baez
defendants.charge: Fraude en adjudicacion de obra publica + lavado de activos (Ruta del Dinero K)
defendants.sentence: Pena unica de 15 anos (unificacion Vialidad 6 anos + Ruta del Dinero K 10 anos)
defendants.status: Condena firme. Preso en Carcel de Rio Gallegos. Decomiso de USD 54,872,866 confirmado.
defendants.additional: Causa por evasion de Austral Construcciones aun abierta (Corte rechazo recurso, marzo 2026)

defendants.name: Jose Lopez
defendants.charge: Administracion fraudulenta (Vialidad) + enriquecimiento ilicito (bolsos) + tenencia de arma

amounts.decomiso_total: ARS 685,000,000,000 (111 propiedades de CFK, hijos y Baez)

cases.connections: Central to Kirchnerism corruption network. Links to Ruta del Dinero K (money laundering of proceeds), Hotesur/Los Sauces (kickback channel), and Cuadernos (broader bribery scheme).

sources.title: Corte Suprema dejo firme condena CFK
CFK pidio frenar decomiso bienes hijos
Casacion mantuvo condena unica 15 anos Baez

cases.summary: Money laundering scheme through which Lazaro Baez laundered proceeds from overpriced public works contracts. Funds moved through shell companies, real estate, and offshore structures.

defendants.name: Martin Baez
defendants.status: Condena firme. Decomiso de bienes confirmado.

cases.connections: Direct sequel to Causa Vialidad. The laundering channel for overpriced public works profits.

sources.title: Corte Suprema dejo firmes condenas Ruta del Dinero K

cases.summary: Money laundering and bribery through Kirchner family companies Hotesur SA and Los Sauces SA. The scheme used fictitious hotel and property rental contracts to channel kickbacks from government contractors (Baez/Austral Construcciones, Cristobal Lopez, Fabian De Sousa) back to the Kirchner family.

defendants.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
defendants.charge: Lavado de activos, cohecho pasivo, asociacion ilicita

defendants.name: Maximo Kirchner
defendants.role: Diputado, hijo de CFK y Nestor Kirchner

defendants.name: Cristobal Lopez
defendants.charge: Participacion en esquema de alquileres ficticios

amounts.note: Pending accounting expert report (peritaje contable) — key evidence not yet completed

cases.connections: Direct kickback channel from Causa Vialidad contractors. Hotesur managed Hotel Alto Calafate; Los Sauces managed other Kirchner properties.

sources.title: Fiscal pidio fijar fecha juicio CFK y Maximo

cases.summary: The largest corruption investigation in Argentine judicial history. Based on notebooks kept by driver Oscar Centeno documenting cash deliveries (coimas) from businessmen to government officials over 12 years. 304 of 540 documented corruption acts being tried. 87 defendants including CFK, 19 former officials, 65 businessmen, and 2 former drivers. 25 arrepentidos (cooperating witnesses).

defendants.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
defendants.charge: Jefa de asociacion ilicita, cohecho pasivo
defendants.status: En juicio oral. Se nego a responder preguntas.

defendants.name: Roberto Baratta
defendants.role: Ex-mano derecha de De Vido en Planificacion Federal

defendants.name: Julio De Vido
defendants.status: En juicio oral. Preso en Ezeiza por condena de Once.

defendants.name: Oscar Centeno
defendants.role: Ex-chofer de Baratta, autor de los cuadernos

defendants.name: 65 empresarios
defendants.status: En juicio oral. 25 son arrepentidos. Algunos ofrecieron pagar USD 12-21M para evitar juicio.
defendants.notable_arrepentidos: Aldo Benito Roggio ofrecio ARS 1,800M para cerrar causa. Enrique Pescarmona (84) sobreseido por Alzheimer.

cases.connections: Overlaps significantly with Causa Vialidad (same corruption network) and Hotesur/Los Sauces (kickback recipients). The cartelization of public works through the Camara Argentina de Empresas Viales is a key element.

sources.title: TOF 7 define si empresarios pagan para evitar juicio
CFK cuestiono investigacion, se nego a responder

cases.court: TOF 2 (Once I) / TOF (Once II - De Vido)
cases.summary: Criminal negligence in the February 22, 2012 train crash at Once station that killed 52 people and injured 789. Government officials allowed TBA (Trenes de Buenos Aires) to operate with dangerously deficient equipment while funneling subsidies that were not used for maintenance.

defendants.name: Ricardo Jaime
defendants.sentence: 6 anos de prision (Once I). Posteriormente aumentada por Casacion.

defendants.name: Julio De Vido
defendants.status: Condena firme. Preso en Complejo Penitenciario Federal I de Ezeiza desde 13/11/2025. Pedido de domiciliaria rechazado. Pedido de prescripcion rechazado.
defendants.other_causes: Tambien condenado a 4 anos por 'trenes chatarra' (compra irregular a Espana/Portugal). Juicio por Cuadernos en curso. Causa Suenos Compartidos en juicio.

amounts.note: Subsidios millonarios a TBA desviados, no utilizados en mantenimiento

cases.connections: De Vido is the common thread — also defendant in Cuadernos, Suenos Compartidos, and convicted for 'trenes chatarra'. Demonstrates pattern of criminal negligence in transport policy for political/financial gain.

sources.title: Corte confirmo condena De Vido 4 anos
De Vido se entrego, trasladado a Ezeiza
De Vido preso y enfermo, pide domiciliaria (Cuadernos)

cases.summary: Former Vice President Amado Boudou orchestrated a scheme to acquire the bankrupt Ciccone Calcografica printing company through front men, then used his position to secure government contracts for printing banknotes and official documents. First vice president in Argentine history convicted of corruption.

defendants.name: Amado Boudou
defendants.charge: Cohecho pasivo (coimas), negociaciones incompatibles con la funcion publica
defendants.sentence: 5 anos y 10 meses de prision (reducida por cursos en detencion)
defendants.status: Libertad condicional desde julio 2021. Tobillera electronica removida. Domiciliaria en Barracas, CABA. Demando al Estado ante la CIDH.
defendants.note: Primer vicepresidente condenado por corrupcion en la historia argentina

amounts.note: Valor de Ciccone Calcografica y contratos de impresion de billetes no cuantificado publicamente en detalle

cases.connections: Boudou was CFK's vice president. The scheme shows the reach of corruption into state monetary functions (printing of currency).

cases.case_name: Suenos Compartidos (Madres de Plaza de Mayo)
cases.summary: Diversion of ARS 206 million (23.54% of total ARS 748M transferred) from the 'Suenos Compartidos' social housing construction program, administered by the Fundacion Madres de Plaza de Mayo under Hebe de Bonafini. Funds meant for building homes for the poor were diverted between March 2008 and June 2011.

defendants.name: Sergio Schoklender
defendants.charge: Administracion fraudulenta en perjuicio de la administracion publica

defendants.name: Julio De Vido
defendants.status: En juicio oral. Ya preso en Ezeiza por causa Once.

defendants.name: Jose Lopez
defendants.status: En juicio oral. Ya condenado (pena unica 13 anos).

defendants.name: Hebe de Bonafini
defendants.role: Presidenta de Madres de Plaza de Mayo (fallecida 2022-11-20)
defendants.status: Fallecida. Procesada en vida pero nunca juzgada.
defendants.note: Schoklender la acuso de usar fondos para campanas de CFK y Boudou

cases.connections: De Vido and Lopez appear here too — same officials from Vialidad and Cuadernos. Bonafini's Madres foundation served as a conduit for political patronage. Schoklender accused Bonafini of channeling funds to CFK/Boudou campaigns.

sources.title: Comenzo juicio Schoklender, Lopez, De Vido
Schoklender acuso a Bonafini de desviar a campanas

cases.summary: On June 14, 2016, former Secretary of Public Works Jose Lopez was arrested at Monasterio Nuestra Senora de Fatima in General Rodriguez while attempting to hide bags containing nearly USD 9 million in cash, jewelry, and a firearm. The case demonstrated the scale of illicit enrichment by K-era officials.

defendants.name: Jose Lopez
defendants.charge: Enriquecimiento ilicito + administracion fraudulenta (Vialidad) + tenencia ilegal de arma
defendants.sentence: Pena unica de 13 anos + multa de 60% del valor del enriquecimiento injustificado + inhabilitacion perpetua especial + inhabilitacion absoluta perpetua

cases.connections: Lopez was a key cog in Vialidad and Cuadernos. Also defendant in Suenos Compartidos. The USD 9M cash is physical evidence of the broader corruption system documented in the Cuadernos.

cases.status: EN ESPERA DE JUICIO ORAL (fecha pendiente, posiblemente 2026)
cases.summary: In January 2013, the Kirchner government signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Iran to create a 'Truth Commission' regarding the 1994 AMIA bombing that killed 85 people. Prosecutor Alberto Nisman accused CFK of negotiating impunity for the Iranian suspects in exchange for favorable trade deals. Nisman was found dead on January 18, 2015, the day before he was to present his case to Congress. The memorandum was approved by the Argentine Congress but never ratified by Iran's parliament, so it never took effect.

defendants.name: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
defendants.charge: Encubrimiento agravado del atentado a la AMIA

amounts.note: No se trata de montos economicos sino de impunidad negociada para sospechosos de terrorismo

cases.connections: Politically the most explosive case. Links K-era foreign policy to alleged obstruction of justice in Argentina's worst terrorist attack. Separate trial in absentia for 10 Iranian/Hezbollah suspects ordered by Judge Rafecas.

sources.title: Justicia pondra fecha juicio oral memorandum
TOF 8 anunciara fecha despues de feria
CFK ira a juicio por encubrimiento AMIA
Rol de Iran en AMIA — batalla judicial

cases.summary: In 2015, PRO/UCR legislators accused CFK, Economy Minister Axel Kicillof, BCRA president Alejandro Vanoli, and CNV president Cristian Girard of fraud for selling dollar futures contracts at ARS 10.6-10.8 when the informal 'blue' dollar was at ARS 16. Expert reports later showed the operations generated gains (ARS 147B in 2015, ARS 67B in 2016) for the Central Bank, not losses.

defendants.name: Axel Kicillof
defendants.role: Ex-Ministro de Economia, actual Gobernador de Buenos Aires

cases.connections: Often cited by Kirchnerism as evidence of 'lawfare' — political use of the judiciary. The case collapsed when expert evidence showed no financial damage to the state.

summary_table.description: Quick-reference status grid for all K-era cases as of March 2026

cross_defendant_map.description: Key defendants and their presence across multiple cases

defendants.name: Lazaro Baez
defendants.current_situation: Preso en Rio Gallegos. Pena unica 15 anos. Evasion Austral abierta.

defendants.name: Julio De Vido
defendants.current_situation: Preso en Ezeiza. Pidio domiciliaria (rechazada) y prescripcion (rechazada).

defendants.name: Jose Lopez
defendants.current_situation: Pena unica 13 anos firme. Tambien arrepentido en Cuadernos.

defendants.name: Amado Boudou
defendants.current_situation: Libertad condicional desde 2021. Demando al Estado ante CIDH.

methodology_notes: Data gathered via web search on 2026-03-21 from Infobae, La Nacion, Chequeado, Fiscales.gob.ar, Palabras del Derecho, and other Argentine legal/news sources.

Status classifications: CONDENA FIRME (final conviction, no appeals remaining), EN JUICIO ORAL (oral trial in progress), ESPERA JUICIO ORAL (awaiting start of oral trial), SOBRESEIMIENTO FIRME (definitively acquitted).
Amounts in ARS reflect nominal values at time of sentencing/decomiso orders; inflation-adjusted equivalents would differ significantly.
Cross-defendant map shows the interlocking nature of K-era corruption: the same ~10 officials and businessmen appear across nearly all cases.
